title: Canonical Serialization Test Vectors
description: Canonical serialization test vectors verifying the rules from wire-format.md Section 3.7 and Section 12.4. Keys are sorted lexicographically (Unicode code point order) at every nesting level. Output is compact (no whitespace). Null/absent fields are omitted. All SHA-256 hashes are computed over the UTF-8 byte representation of the canonical JSON string.

serializationRules.rule1: Object keys MUST be sorted lexicographically (Unicode code point order) at every nesting level
serializationRules.rule2: No whitespace between tokens (compact form: separators are ',' and ':')
serializationRules.rule3: Null/absent optional fields MUST be omitted (not serialized as null)
serializationRules.rule4: The resulting UTF-8 byte sequence is the canonical form
serializationRules.rule5: UUIDs are lowercase hex with hyphens
serializationRules.rule6: Timestamps use ISO 8601 with exactly 3 fractional digits and Z suffix
serializationRules.rule7: Integer values have no fractional parts
serializationRules.rule8: payerResponsibilityRatio is a JSON number with at most 4 decimal places

testCases.description: CreateEvent with minimal fields (no optional fields). Verifies that optional fields (time, location, notes) are omitted, not serialized as null.
notes: Optional fields (time, location, notes) are NOT present in the output
Keys are alphabetically sorted: childId < date < entityId < eventId < operationType < payloadType < timestamp < title

testCases.description: CreateEvent with ALL optional fields present. Verifies that optional fields are included when present and sorted among other keys.
testCases.expectedCanonicalJson: {"childId":"c1d2e3f4-5678-9abc-def0-123456789012","date":"2026-04-10","entityId":"11223344-5566-7788-99aa-bbccddeeff00","eventId":"11223344-5566-7788-99aa-bbccddeeff00","location":"Grundschule am Park, Room 201","notes":"Bring last semester's report card.","operationType":"CREATE","payloadType":"CreateEvent","time":"15:30:00","timestamp":"2026-03-16T12:00:00.000Z","title":"Parent-teacher conference"}
notes: location sorts between eventId and notes
notes sorts between location and operationType
time sorts between payloadType and timestamp

testCases.description: CreateExpense with numeric precision edge case. payerResponsibilityRatio of 0.6 must serialize as '0.6' (not '0.60' or '0.6000').
CancelEvent with very minimal fields. Smallest possible OperationPayload.
notes: entityId sorts before eventId (third char 't' vs 'v')
operationType is DELETE (used only by CancelEvent)

testCases.description: UpdateExpenseStatus WITH optional note field. Verifies that the note field is included when present.

input.note: Amount seems too high for a standard visit. Can you share the invoice?

testCases.expectedCanonicalJson: {"entityId":"a1234567-b890-cdef-1234-567890abcdef","expenseId":"a1234567-b890-cdef-1234-567890abcdef","note":"Amount seems too high for a standard visit. Can you share the invoice?","operationType":"UPDATE","payloadType":"UpdateExpenseStatus","responderId":"e2f3a4b5-6789-abcd-ef01-bbbbbbbbbbbb","status":"DISPUTED","timestamp":"2026-03-19T09:00:00.000Z"}
notes: note field is present and sorts between expenseId and operationType
status is an enum string in UPPERCASE

testCases.description: UpdateExpenseStatus WITHOUT optional note field. Verifies that the note field is omitted (not null).
notes: note field is absent (NOT serialized as 'note':null)
Compare with CS-05: same payload type but note is omitted, producing a different hash
Only 7 fields vs 8 fields in CS-05

testCases.description: CreateExpense with receipt blob fields. Verifies that receiptBlobId and receiptDecryptionKey are included and sorted correctly.
notes: receiptBlobId sorts after payloadType and before receiptDecryptionKey
receiptDecryptionKey is a base64 string (not raw bytes in the JSON)
Both receipt fields are present together (dependentRequired)

antiPatterns.description: Examples of INCORRECT canonical serialization that implementations MUST NOT produce

examples.error: Null field included instead of omitted
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.3: Fields with null values MUST be omitted
wire-format.md Section 3.7: Object keys MUST be sorted lexicographically
wire-format.md Section 3.7: No whitespace between tokens (compact form)
wire-format.md Section 3.6: Integer values without fractional parts; ratio with minimal precision

examples.error: Timestamp missing milliseconds or Z suffix
examples.rule: wire-format.md Section 3.4: Instant values MUST include exactly 3 fractional digits
wire-format.md Section 3.5: UUID lowercase hex with hyphens
